``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined The length of minimum terms in relation to judicial maximum sentences is entirely a matter of discretion vested in sentencing courts. Credit-based deductions from both minimum and maximum terms are statutorily capped at 35 percent.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were 1,339 people in prison past their parole consideration year. This group made up 28% of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
### Demographics The demographics of people in prison past their parole consideration year are mostly notable proportions among Hispanic, any race and White, non-Hispanic people. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
### Criminal Offenses ::: {.cell} ::: In 2020, 52% of people in prison past their parole consideration year were in prison for non-violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses reveals a more varied landscape, with most people incarcerated for drugs (29%) and robbery (14%) offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
### Original Sentence Lengths In 2020, most people in prison past their parole consideration year had original sentence lengths between 2 to 4.9 years representing 45% of those eligible for parole. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Missing Data This section will contain information related to missingness in the NCRP data. Keep?


# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population decreased 46%, changing from 11,312 in 2010 to 6,148 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners Series
## Demographics The demographics of people in prison are mostly Data on race and ethnicity is incomplete or missing. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners 2022 - Race and Ethnicity
## Criminal Offenses In 2020, 72% of people in prison were incarcerated for violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses reveals a more varied landscape, with most people incarcerated for murder and non-negligent manslaughter (34%) and rape or sexual assault (15%) offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Sentence Lengths In 2020, most people in prison had original sentence lengths between Life, LWOP, Life plus additional years, Death representing 27%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2020, prison releases decreased 44%, dropping from 3,794 in 2010 to 2,124 in 2020. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
In 2020, 42% of people eligible for parole were released during their eligibility year. This represents a 21% decrease compared to 2010. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Demographics of People Released The demographics of people released from prison are mostly notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and hispanic, any race people. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. These findings provide insights into the populations transitioning back into the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served by Offense Between 2010 and 2020, there were shifts in the average time served by individuals for different offense types in Massachusetts. The largest change was for property offenses, which increased by 130%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Disparities ## Incarceration Rates In Massachusetts, Black, non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 7.8 times higher and Hispanic, any race people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 4.1 times higher than White, non-Hispanic people, when accounting for population sizes in the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Years Spent in Prison After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity In 2020, people in prison past their parole eligibility year collectively spent a total of 165 years behind bars instead of supervised in the community on parole.

## Timing of Release After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity The timing of release for individuals after their parole eligibility year varied by offense type and demographic group, with many remaining in prison for several years beyond their eligibility date. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender In progress. Same type of graph as race and ethnicity tab. :::
## Time Served ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: Other races people faced the longest average time served in prison in 2020, with an average of 4.7 years. Hispanic people experienced shorter prison stays, averaging 3.7 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: By offense type, disparities were observed in time served by race and ethnicity. For rape or sexual assault offenses, Black people had 3.6 more years on average compared to Hispanic people, who had the shortest time served for these offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender In progress. Same type of graph as race and ethnicity tab. :::
